question: Is the airborne basketball positioned closer to the hoop rim, appearing as if it is about to drop in?
Has the defender on the right been shifted approximately half a body width to the right?
Is the defender on the right now depicted in a full jump with both feet clearly off the ground?
Is the right-side defender's palm positioned closer to the basketball without actually touching it?
Do the floor reflections and shadows beneath the right-side defender correctly track and align with his new jumping position?
Does the jumping defender maintain natural anatomical proportions without distorted limbs or floating parts?
Does the crowd in the stands remain sharp and free from blur or distortion?
Have all other players (including the red jersey shooter and Duke #5) remained in their original positions and poses?
Is the basketball net and backboard preserved in its original position without distortion?
Is the floor area where the defender was originally standing free from ghosting, residual artifacts, or texture discontinuities?
Is the image free from visible seams, halos, or unnatural blending artifacts around the moved basketball and defender?
Are the lighting, motion blur, and resolution on the moved basketball and defender consistent with the rest of the scene?
